reason: Both findings concern `StringUtil` missing from hints in s1 and s2. `StringUtil` is a generic Nablarch utility class, not specific to language/timezone configuration. Its absence from hints does not prevent retrieval of these sections — users looking for language or timezone configuration would search using the many domain-specific hints already present (e.g., `keepLanguage`, `I18nHandler`, `言語切替`). Adding `StringUtil` as a hint could even cause false positives. The actual code examples in the section bodies already contain `StringUtil`, so the knowledge itself is complete. Phase E did not apply the fix because the change offers negligible retrieval benefit and would add noise to the hint index.

findings_assessment.justification: `StringUtil` is a general-purpose utility class unrelated to the core subject of s1 (LanguageAttribute configuration). Users querying about language switching would not use `StringUtil` as a search term. The full code example including `StringUtil.hasValue(language)` is already present in the section body, so the knowledge is accessible. Omitting it from hints has no practical retrieval impact. Phase E left it unchanged because adding a generic utility class name as a domain hint provides no meaningful benefit and risks polluting retrieval for unrelated `StringUtil` queries.

Identical reasoning to s1: `StringUtil` is a cross-cutting utility, not a TimeZoneAttribute-specific class. The code example `StringUtil.hasValue(timeZone)` is already visible in the section body. Retrieval for timezone configuration scenarios relies on purpose-specific hints already present (`keepTimeZone`, `タイムゾーン切替`, `I18nHandler`). Phase E did not apply the fix for the same reason as s1 — the hint adds noise without improving targeted retrieval.
